description: Dicranoloma dicarpum is relatively common moss which is widespread in the Southern Hemisphere. The genus Dicranoloma has 40 species, which share the features of long stems, wispy and twisted leaves, and large, erect capsules. The genus is dominant in wet forest habitats in Australia and New Zealand.
Dicranum drummondii is a species of moss belonging to the family Dicranaceae. It is native to Northern Hemisphere.
Dicranum majus is a species of moss belonging to the family Dicranaceae.

ja: ディクラヌム・モンタヌム

description: Dicranum scoparium, the broom forkmoss, is a dicranaceous moss, native to most of the northern hemisphere as well as Oceania. It usually forms and grows in round mass clumps or mats on soil in dry to moist forested areas. As with many types of moss Broom moss grows in clumps with Broom mosses as well as other mosses. It can be distinguished by its leaves, which strongly curve to one side.
Dicranum spurium is a species of moss belonging to the family Dicranaceae.

Dicranum tauricum is a species of fork moss in the family Dicranaceae. It is a native to western North America and has been introduced to Europe. The male plants are the same size as the females. They can be found in lowland forests on rotting logs or tree stumps.
Dicranum viride is a species of moss belonging to the family Dicranaceae.
Oncophorus virens is a species of moss in the genus Oncophorus native to Alaska.
Paraleucobryum longifolium is a species of moss belonging to the family Dicranaceae.
